nodes.label: Add Automation Job
nodes.intent: A new recurring job is added to the platform automation.

nodes.label: Allow Resource Type
nodes.intent: A resource type is admitted to the platform only after security controls, diagnostic settings, and access roles are fully wired.

nodes.label: Create Landing Zone
nodes.intent: A new isolated Azure environment is provisioned under the application team's billing scope, with guardrails pre-wired and ready for workload deployment.

nodes.label: Destroy Landing Zone
nodes.intent: A landing zone is permanently torn down, all resources destroyed, and the subscription returned to the subscription-bank — no manual cleanup left behind.

nodes.label: Register Application Profile
nodes.intent: An application team is registered with the platform, giving them a billing scope and a GitHub repo — the prerequisite for full self-service control over their landing zones.

nodes.label: Update Knowledge Base
nodes.intent: The knowledge graph remains accurate, consistent, and self-contained — every entry follows the schema, links are organic and directional, and the validation script passes before any PR is opened.

nodes.label: Update Landing Zone
nodes.intent: An existing landing zone is updated — cost ceiling, tags, or policy exemptions — entirely through the repo, with no portal access required.

nodes.label: Application Teams Own The Cost
nodes.decision: Each application has its own billing scope; every subscription it provisions bills to it exclusively.

nodes.label: Azure Policy Allowed Resources
nodes.decision: Every resource is denied by default. It appears on the allowed list only after the platform has approved it.

nodes.label: Azure Policy Custom Definition
nodes.decision: When no built-in policy provides the required Deny or DeployIfNotExists effect, a custom definition is authored to close the gap.

nodes.label: Azure Policy Reference
nodes.decision: Policy exemptions source assignment IDs from the generated reference file.

nodes.label: Branch Gated Environment Promotion
nodes.decision: Every platform production deployment passes through a test environment first. This is enforced by branch protection rules and workflow structure.

nodes.label: Declarative Resource Lifecycle
nodes.decision: What's in the code is what exists in Azure — Azure Deployment Stacks delete anything removed from the template on the next deployment.

nodes.label: Deny Security Controls
nodes.decision: Azure Policy enforces hard denies on public network access, local authentication, minimum TLS, and cross-tenant replication, on every resource type that supports them.

nodes.label: End To End Deployment
nodes.decision: Each platform concern (policy, access control, management groups) owns its Bicep and workflows end-to-end.

nodes.label: Ipam
nodes.decision: When a new landing zone is requested, the GitHub workflow queries Azure Resource Graph and finds the next free VNet address space. The address space is written to a landing zone parameter file.

nodes.label: Job Function Scoped Roles
nodes.decision: Roles are scoped to a job function. A role describes what a person does (e.g., app engineer), not what resources they touch.

nodes.label: Landing Zone Application Profile
nodes.decision: Registering an Application Profile is how an application joins the platform and gains the ability to provision its own landing zones.

nodes.label: Landing Zone Automation
nodes.decision: Each landing zone has automation runtime running inside its own subscription — job definitions and images are centrally managed by the platform, but execution happens locally.

nodes.label: Landing Zone Diagnostic Settings
nodes.decision: Every whitelisted resource type has diagnostic settings deployed automatically via DeployIfNotExists policy. Telemetry flows to the landing zone's Log Analytics workspace with no team action required.

nodes.label: Landing Zone Identity
nodes.decision: Each landing zone has a single managed identity that serves all its capabilities — GitHub workflows, automation jobs, and Entra ID queries.

nodes.label: Landing Zone Lifecycle
nodes.decision: Landing zone provisioning draws from a subscription bank, returning the subscription to it when the landing zone is sunset.

nodes.label: Landing Zone Template
nodes.decision: Landing zones are stamped from a single shared template, but each is managed through its own dedicated parameter file and workflow.

nodes.label: Platform Exemptions Automation
nodes.decision: The platform uses GitHub Container Registry as the shared image source for all automation jobs across all landing zones.

nodes.label: Self Service Policy Exemptions
nodes.decision: Application teams can request a temporary or long-lived exemption from a specific Azure Policy assignment via a self-service workflow.

nodes.label: Self Service Policy Exemptions Defender For Cloud
nodes.decision: Defender for Cloud recommendations that require a paid SKU are exempted by the platform.
